mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 65 percent of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate profit margins contract, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum and capacity utilization gains, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns show no directional bias following positive INDPRO prints, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices and elevated policy rates creates a real rate compression effect that erodes equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises consistently coincide with negative equity returns, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent, aggregate demand outpaces baseline expectations, driving corporate profit expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline following strong GDP prints, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment signals productive capacity expansion without labor market overheating, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern coincides with equity drawdowns, or the signal fails to generate statistically significant alpha with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample periods.
